mechanism_narrative: The Federal Reserve has maintained an exceptionally low federal funds rate (0.54% as of June 2022) while inflation (CPI 242.348) has surged. This creates a negative real rate environment that incentivizes portfolio reallocation away from bonds toward equities. As market participants recognize the unsustainability of this policy stance, expectations of imminent rate hikes drive 10-year Treasury yields upward over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Real rates deeply negative in mid-2022
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation shock
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over 20-day forward period despite negative real rate signal, or Rank IC < 0.05 over rolling 12-month windows during periods when FEDFUNDS < 1% and CPIAUCSL > 240.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.7% signals a tight labor market with wage pressure building. Sustained low unemployment combined with elevated payroll employment (145,303K) indicates the Fed will continue aggressive rate hikes to combat inflation, pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher as market reprices terminal rate expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:Labor market remains resilient despite Fed tightening
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline over 20-day period despite UNRATE < 5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window when UNRATE is below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS at 469,092M) remain elevated despite Fed tightening, indicating consumer demand has not yet rolled over. This persistent demand growth forces the Fed to maintain higher rates for longer, pushing 10-year Treasury yields upward as the terminal rate expectation rises and the duration of elevated rates extends.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending remains resilient in Q2 2022
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields fall over 5-day period despite RSAFS > 460000, or Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS and 10Y yield changes is negative (< -0.10) over any 6-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO at 103.86) shows supply-side constraints persist. When supply growth lags demand growth, inflation remains sticky, requiring the Fed to signal higher terminal rates. This forward guidance pushes 10-year Treasury yields higher as markets price in a longer period of restrictive monetary policy to restore price stability.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline over 60-day period when INDPRO < 105 and RSAFS > 460000, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window in supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST at 1,090K) have begun to decelerate from cycle peaks as mortgage rates rise in anticipation of Fed tightening. This demand destruction in the housing sector signals the beginning of a demand-side slowdown that will eventually reduce inflation pressure, allowing the Fed to pause rate hikes sooner than currently expected, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise over 20-day period when HOUST < 1100, or Rank IC > -0.05 (i.e., signal is not negatively correlated with yields) over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The Fed funds rate at 0.54% is far below the inflation rate (CPI 242.348 implies ~8% YoY inflation). This extreme policy accommodation gap forces the Fed into a rapid tightening cycle. Market participants front-run this tightening by selling long-duration bonds, pushing 10-year Treasury yields sharply higher as the market reprices the entire yield curve upward.
evidence_refs: fact:Real federal funds rate deeply negative
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline over 5-day period when FEDFUNDS < 1%, or average 5-day yield change is negative in > 40% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 1%.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS at 145,303K) continues to grow despite Fed rate hikes, indicating labor market resilience. This persistent employment growth prevents the Fed from achieving its inflation target through demand destruction, forcing the central bank to signal an even higher terminal rate, which pushes 10-year Treasury yields upward as the market extends its rate hike expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:Labor market remains tight despite Fed tightening
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline over 60-day period when PAYEMS > 145000 and UNRATE < 5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window in tight labor market regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1 at 16,727B) remains elevated, but the combination of rising rates and housing demand destruction signals the economy is entering a slowdown phase. As growth expectations decline, the term premium on 10-year Treasuries compresses, causing yields to fall as investors reduce their inflation and growth risk premiums.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing demand declining while GDP remains elevated
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise over 60-day period when HOUST < 1100, or Rank IC > -0.04 over any 3-year rolling window during housing demand destruction phases.
